response.justification: The candidate is a strong, seasoned individual contributor with deep developer-tooling and testing-infrastructure experience (built the Trial test runner and Fluent-Asserts, set up GitLab/CI/CD pipelines, MCP code generators) that maps well to the engineering-productivity focus. However, this is a people-leadership role requiring 2+ years of formal people management, and the resume shows no evidence of managing engineers, owning headcount, running performance reviews, or leading organizational change — every title is an IC role. The technical depth is there; the management track record is essentially absent, which is the core requirement.
strengths: Extensive developer-tooling and test-automation work (Trial runner, Fluent-Asserts, CI/CD, GitLab infra) directly relevant to engineering productivity
10+ years of broad software development across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Demonstrated mentoring/teaching via workshops, dojos, and courses, plus some mobile exposure (iOS/Apple TV apps)
concerns: No formal people-management or team-leadership experience to meet the 2-year minimum
No evidence of leading organizational change or owning team roadmap/performance processes
Limited large-scale mobile-platform ecosystem and build-systems-at-scale experience for the specific focus area
